meaning: adj.记在页边的,边缘的,边际的
examples: High marginal tax rates may act as a disincentive to working longer hours.
The analysts applaud the cuts in marginal businesses, but insist the company must make deeper sacrifices.
To call something "marginal" means it is not very good.
And if you think James would have made more than a marginal difference this year, think again.
The marginal rate went down, then partially up, and the capital-gains rate did the reverse.
